title: Entity 110
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Entity 110: "The Unfinished Symphony"}}__NOTOC__{{Blurbackground|file=Entity110.jpg}}
{{Entity Class|Agg=4|no=110|Int=5|Dan=5|Hab=Nearly Extinct, Most reported presence of Entity 110 at Level 2}} == Description == '''Entity 110''', also known as "The Unfinished Symphony", is a complex, exposed-chassis biomechanical structure, approximately 2 meters in height. '''Entity 110''' is fundamentally composed of a mismatched, jury-rigged amalgamation of industrial actuators, pistons, hydraulic lines, and a massive internal network of power conduits that lack a unifying schematical pattern. These components are secured to a central spinal frame that exhibits signs of heavy corrosion and systemic electrical arcing, yet the structure remains entirely self-sustaining. '''Entity 110''' is perpetually suspended from a ceiling by a bundle of thick, severed data cables that are actively pulling against its own gravitational force. {{Blurimage|width=250px|align=Right|file=Entity110.jpg|caption=An image of '''Entity 110''' in Unknown Level.}} '''Entity 110'''’s primary sensory array is located where a head would be but instead consists of a cluster of multiple, multi-focal lens asemblies and exposed CCD sensors, giving the impression of an arachnid visual system rather than a human one. Directly above this lens cluster is a single, cracked CRT monitor. This monitor does not display a face, but rather a constant, scrolling feed of hexadecimal error codes and fragmented audio-reactive waveforms. The monitor has been observed to change colors from a green to a purple, a visual cue that correlates with shifts in '''Entity 110'''’s acoustic and thermals output. The lower limbs of '''Entity 110''' are engineered for stability, utilizing oversized pistons and reinforced feet. However, it does not move by walking. Instead, it seems to "skate" and maintain balance by interacting with the cylindrical object it was first discovered on. This object is a massive, hollow structural drum made of a ceramic-polymer composite, possibly a part of the original machine '''Entity 110''' was synthesized from. The drum itself emits a constant, high-frequency kinetic vibration and is considered an extension of '''Entity 110'''. To date, no force has been able to separate '''Entity 110''' from this platform without catastrophic failure of the localized area. Structurally, '''Entity 110''' is in a state of suspended decay. Thousands of wires and cables are not integrated into the body but dangle in loose, tangled loops, trailing for meters behind and beneath it like a mechanical root system. Many of these wires are actively arcing with current, while others seem to have "fused" with the surrounding environment, turning the ceiling, walls, and floor within a 3-meter radius into a chaotic network of parasitic conduits. In areas where '''Entity 110''' has been stationary for extended periods, the architecture itself has begun to mimic '''Entity 110'''’s wiring, with concrete cracking into patterns identical to its circuitry. == Behavior == '''Entity 110''' is primarily an environment-altering, passive-aggressive. It spends 90% of its observed active time in a state of chaotic, repetitive vibration, appearing to "read" or "transmit" data through the cylindrical drum it stands on. It does not navigate; it assimilates. '''Entity 110''' will "anchor" itself to a location, and its dangling wires will slowly creep outward. Once these wires make contact with the environment (metal, concrete, or electronic), '''Entity 110''' begins to "overwrite" the material's properties, effectively converting the physical surroundings into a massive, inefficient power conduit. Aggression is non-traditional. It will not physically chase or strike a subject. Instead, its host's hostility manifests as a severe, acute disruption of the local physics and electrical grids. If a wanderer approaches within 10 meters, '''Entity 110''' will immediately enter a "Transmission Cycle." The CRT monitor will flash purple, and '''Entity 110'''’s acoustic hum will increase from 60 Hz to 20,000+ Hz, causing instantaneous nausea, vestibular disruption, and a severe reduction in cognitive function in all organic subjects. Electronics are fried instantly by massive electromagnetic pulses. '''Entity 110''' displays no response to verbal communication, flashlights, or other standard interaction. The only stimulus that has consistently altered its behavioral loop is acoustic. It will cease all assimilation activity and entry into aggression cycles if exposed to a complex, harmonious melody (such as a piece of classical music played correctly on an acoustic instrument). During this rare "receptive state," the error feed on the monitor will slow, the purple flickering will stop, and '''Entity 110''' has been seen to shift its lens arrays toward the sound source in an act of apparent observation. == Biology == '''Entity 110''' is a structural aberration that defies classification. It is a fusion of late 20th-century animatronics, discarded industrial hardware, and a highly invasive, synthetic nervous system of unknown origin. Dissection attempts have been fatal; '''Entity 110'''’s cables are highly pressurized and will release a cocktail of hydraulic fluid, cooling agents, and a dense, black grahitic powder that, when inhaled, will cause pulmonary paralysis by binding directly to lung tissue. The internal chassis is entirely hollow, a chaotic cave of tangled, energized conduits. There is no "brain." It is theorized that '''Entity 110'''’s consciousness is decentralized, distributed throughout its thousands of cables, making the elimination of a single "core" impossible. The CRT monitor appears to be the only point of centralized data display, but smashing the screen only results in the waveform feed migrating to the lens cluster, which will then use its focus elements to project the error codes onto the surrounding walls. '''Entity 110''' does not require organic sustenance but instead absorbs the raw electromagnetic energy from the levels it assimilates, growing more robust the more environment it corrupts. == Discovery and History == '''Entity 110''' occurred during a routine sweep of a collapsed maintenance sector in Level 2. Explorers from a local reconnaissance group reported a localized failure of all recording equipment, followed by a persistent, rhythmic thumping sound that vibrated through the reinforced floorboards. Upon entering a high-security R&D chamber, the team discovered '''Entity 110''' suspended in a state of violent oscillation, its multiple lenses tracking their thermal signatures before any light source was even directed toward it. The room was found to be physically "warped," with the industrial wiring of the facility having been forcibly rerouted into '''Entity 110'''’s own chassis. During the first contact, the reconnaissance team attempted to sever the trailing data cables to free what they initially believed to be a trapped human in an experimental suit. This action triggered an immediate atmospheric ionization event, resulting in the instantaneous incineration of the team's lead technician. The remaining members reported that '''Entity 110''' did not move toward them, but rather the floor beneath their feet began to liquefy into a black, conductive sludge that mimicked '''Entity 110'''’s internal hexadecimal output. This event confirmed that '''Entity 110'''’s influence was not limited to its physical frame but extended to the very molecular structure of the surrounding architecture. Fragmented records recovered from the surrounding archives suggest that '''Entity 110''' was the result of a catastrophic logic breach during an early attempt to automate localized facility mapping. Project logs refer to a "Symphony Protocol" designed to use decentralized sensors to stabilize the shifting geometry of the sector's hallways. It is hypothesized that the protocol encountered a recursive error, causing the hardware to begin cannibalizing nearby machinery and organic matter to build a more stable processor. The result was a feedback loop that turned a mapping tool into a predatory, self-assimilating mass. By the time the wing was abandoned, '''Entity 110''' had already expanded through several sub-basements, turning them into a chaotic web of energized conduits and data-leaching traps. Early attempts by organized groups to decommission '''Entity 110''' resulted in total losses of entire tactical squads. These failures are attributed to '''Entity 110'''’s ability to read the tactical software of the squads and overwrite their communication channels with the purple hexadecimal flare. History logs indicate that '''Entity 110'''’s presence was the primary reason for the permanent sealing of the wing, as the cost of containment outweighed the value of the reclaimed territory. In the years following the facility's total abandonment, '''Entity 110''' has shown a disturbing trend of internal expansion. While the main chassis remains anchored in the R&D chamber, the frequency emitted by '''Entity 110''' has begun to appear in distant corridors of the same facility. This suggests that '''Entity 110''' is successfully broadcasting its own blueprint across the internal electrical grid, essentially infecting new rooms with its corrupted architecture. == Do and Don't == === Do === Break all line of sight with '''Entity 110'''’s CRT monitor immediately upon visual confirmation. Drop all electronic equipment and cover your ears. Use a UV burst or high-intensity floodlight only to temporarily disorient its sensors during retreat. Retreat with your eyes locked on '''Entity 110'''’s visual array. === Don't === Approaching '''Entity 110''' within 10 meters, regardless of '''Entity 110'''’s apparent dormancy. Touching any dangling, sparking, or loose wires trailing on the ground. Attempting to separate '''Entity 110''' from the large drum-like platform it rests on. Shouting or use verbal language in its presence. Using any magnetic tools near '''Entity 110'''.
